name: All Fours Quad Stretch
steps: Start off on your hands and knees, then lift your leg off the floor and hold the foot with your hand.
Use your hand to hold the foot or ankle, keeping the knee fully flexed, stretching the quadriceps and hip flexors.
Focus on extending your hips, thrusting them towards the floor. Hold for 10-20 seconds and then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for All Fours Quad Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall
steps: Stand facing a wall from a couple feet away.
Lean against the wall, placing your weight on your forearms.
Attempt to keep your heels on the ground. Hold for 10-20 seconds. You may move further or closer the wall, making it more or less difficult, respectively.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Calf Stretch Hands Against Wall
steps: Stand facing a wall from several feet away. Stagger your stance, placing one foot forward.
Lean forward and rest your hands on the wall, keeping your heel, hip and head in a straight line.
Attempt to keep your heel on the ground. Hold for 10-20 seconds and then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Calf Stretch Hands Against Wall, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Hamstring Stretch
steps: Lie on your back with one leg extended above you, with the hip at ninety degrees. Keep the other leg flat on the floor.
Loop a belt, band, or rope over the ball of your foot. This will be your starting position.
Pull on the belt to create tension in the calves and hamstrings. Hold this stretch for 10-30 seconds, and repeat with the other leg.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Hamstring Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Intermediate Groin Stretch
steps: Lie on your back with your legs extended. Loop a belt, rope, or band around one of your feet, and swing that leg as far to the side as you can. This will be your starting position.
Pull gently on the belt to create tension in your groin and hamstring muscles. Hold for 10-20 seconds, and repeat on the other side.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Intermediate Groin Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Intermediate Hip Flexor and Quad Stretch
steps: Lie face down on the floor, with a rope, belt, or band looped around one foot.

Flex the knee and extend the hip of the leg to be stretched, using both hands to pull on the belt. Your knee and your hip should come off of the floor, creating tension in the hip flexors and quadriceps. Hold the stretch for 10-20 seconds, and repeat on the other leg.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Intermediate Hip Flexor and Quad Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Iron Crosses (stretch)
steps: Lie face down on the floor, with your arms extended out to your side, palms pressed to the floor. This will be your starting position.
To begin, flex one knee and bring that leg across the back of your body, attempting to touch it to the ground near the opposite hand.
Promptly return the leg to the starting postion, and quickly repeat with the other leg. Continue alternating for 10-20 repetitions.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Iron Crosses (stretch), mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: IT Band and Glute Stretch
steps: Loop a belt, rope, or band around one of your feet, and swing that leg across your body to the opposite side, keeping the leg extended as you lay on the ground. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your foot off of the floor, pull on the belt, using the tension to pull the toes up. Hold for 10-20 seconds, and repeat on the other side.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Check the band and anchor before starting.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for IT Band and Glute Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Leg-Up Hamstring Stretch
steps: Lie flat on your back, bend one knee, and put that foot flat on the floor to stabilize your spine.

Extend the other leg in the air. If you're tight, you wont be able to straighten it. That's okay. Extend the knee so that the sole of the lifted foot faces the ceiling (or as close as you can get it).
Slowly straighten the legs as much as possible and then pull the leg toward your nose. Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Leg-Up Hamstring Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: On Your Side Quad Stretch
steps: Start off by lying on your right side, with your right knee bent at a 90-degree angle resting on the floor in front of you (this stabilizes the torso).
Bend your left knee behind you and hold your left foot with your left hand. To stretch your hip flexor, press your left hip forward as you push your left foot back into your hand. Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for On Your Side Quad Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Peroneals Stretch
steps: In a seated position, loop a belt, rope, or band around one foot. This will be your starting position.

With the leg extended and the heel off of the ground, pull on the belt so that the foot is inverted, with the inside of the foot being pulled towards you. Hold for 10-20 seconds, and then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Peroneals Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Posterior Tibialis Stretch
steps: In a seated position, loop a belt, rope, or band around one foot. This will be your starting position.

With the leg extended and the heel off of the ground, pull on the belt so that the foot is everted, with the outside of the foot being pulled towards you. Hold for 10-20 seconds, and then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Posterior Tibialis Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Quad Stretch
steps: Lay on your side. Loop a belt, rope, or band around your top foot. Flex the knee and extend your hip, attempting to touch your glutes with your foot, and holding the belt with your hands. This will be your starting position.
With the belt being held over the shoulder or overhead, gently pull to increase the stretch in the quadriceps. Hold for 10-20 seconds, and then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Quad Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Runner's Stretch
steps: It's easiest to get into this stretch if you start standing up, put one leg behind you, and slowly lower your torso down to the floor.
Keep the front heel on the floor (if it lifts up, scoot your other leg further back).

Place your hands on either side of your front leg. To get more out of this stretch, push your butt up toward the ceiling, and then gradually lower it back toward the floor. You'll Stretch the hip flexor of the back leg and the hamstring and buttocks of the front.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Runner's Stretch, mobility hips movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Chair Lower Back Stretch
steps: Sit upright on a chair.
Bend to one side with your arm over your head. You can hold onto the chair with your free hand.
Hold for 10 seconds, and repeat for your other side.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Chair Lower Back Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Dancer's Stretch
steps: Sit up on the floor.
Cross your right leg over your left, keeping the knee bent. Your left leg is straight and down on the floor.
Place your left arm on your right leg and your right hand on the floor.
Rotate your upper body to the right, and hold for 10-20 seconds. Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Dancer's Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Dynamic Back Stretch
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your arms straight, swing them straight up in front of you 5-10 times, increasing the range of motion each time until your arms are above your head.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Dynamic Back Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Groin and Back Stretch
steps: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet together.
Interlock your fingers behind your head. This will be your starting position.
Curl downwards, bringing your elbows to the inside of your thighs. After a brief pause, return to the starting position with your head up and your back straight. Repeat for 10-20 repetitions.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Groin and Back Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Middle Back Stretch
steps: Stand so your feet are shoulder width apart and your hands are on your hips.
Twist at your waist until you feel a stretch. Hold for 10 to 15 seconds, then twist to the other side.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Middle Back Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: On-Your-Back Quad Stretch
steps: Lie on a flat bench or step, and hang one leg and arm over the side.
Bend the knee and hold the top of the foot. As you do this, be careful not to arch your lower back.
Pull the belly button to the spine to stay in neutral. Press your foot down and into your hand. To add the hip stretch, lift the hip of the leg you're holding up toward the ceiling.
Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for On-Your-Back Quad Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Pelvic Tilt Into Bridge
steps: Lie down with your feet on the floor, heels directly under your knees.
Lift only your tailbone to the ceiling to stretch your lower back. (Don't lift the entire spine yet.) Pull in your stomach.
To go into a bridge, lift the entire spine except the neck.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Pelvic Tilt Into Bridge, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Seated Calf Stretch
steps: Sit up straight on an exercise mat.
Bend one knee and put that foot on the floor to stabilize the torso.
Straighten your other leg and flex your ankle.
Using a band, towel, or your hand if you can reach, pull the toes toward you. Hold for 10 to 20 seconds, then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Seated Calf Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Side-Lying Floor Stretch
steps: First lie on your left side, bending your left knee in front of you to stabilize your torso (use your abdominal muscles as well to hold you upright).

Straighten your right leg and rest the right foot on the floor behind your left. Straighten your right arm over your head and gently pull on your right wrist to stretch the entire right side of the body. Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Side-Lying Floor Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Triceps Stretch
steps: Reach your hand behind your head, grasp your elbow and gently pull. Hold for 10 to 20 seconds, then switch sides.
easier_version: Use a smaller, slower, pain-free range.
harder_version: Add a longer pause while keeping the movement calm and pain-free.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Triceps Stretch, mobility spine movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Band Hip Adductions
steps: Anchor a band around a solid post or other object.
Stand with your left side to the post, and put your right foot through the band, getting it around the ankle.
Stand up straight and hold onto the post if needed. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the knee straight, raise your right legs out to the side as far as you can.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired rep count.
Switch sides.
easier_version: Use a lighter band or stand closer to the anchor point.
harder_version: Use a stronger band, step farther from the anchor, or add a pause.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
Check the band and anchor before starting.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Band Hip Adductions, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Calf Raise On A Dumbbell
steps: Hang on to a sturdy object for balance and stand on a dumbbell handle, preferably one with round plates so that it rolls as in this manner you have to work harder to stabilize yourself; thus increasing the effectiveness of the exercise.
Now roll your foot slightly forward so that you can get a nice stretch of the calf. This will be your starting position.

Lift the calf as you roll your foot over the top of the handle so that you get a full extension. Exhale during the execution of this movement. Contract the calf hard at the top and hold for a second. Tip: As you come up, roll the dumbbell slightly backward.
Now inhale as you roll the dumbbell slightly forward as you come down to get a better stretch.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
easier_version: Use a lighter weight and a smaller range of motion.
harder_version: Use a slightly heavier load, add a pause, or slow the lowering phase.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Calf Raise On A Dumbbell, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Calf Raises - With Bands
steps: Grab an exercise band and stand on it with your toes making sure that the length of the band between the foot and the arms is the same for both sides.

While holding the handles of the band, raise the arms to the side of your head as if you were getting ready to perform a shoulder press. The palms should be facing forward with the elbows bent and to the sides. This movement will create tension on the band. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the hands by your shoulder, stand up on your toes as you exhale and contract the calves hard at the top of the movement.
After a one second contraction, slowly go back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
easier_version: Use a lighter band or stand closer to the anchor point.
harder_version: Use a stronger band, step farther from the anchor, or add a pause.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
Check the band and anchor before starting.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Calf Raises - With Bands, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Dumbbell Seated One-Leg Calf Raise
steps: Place a block on the floor about 12 inches from a flat bench.
Sit on a flat bench and place a dumbbell on your upper left thigh about 3 inches above your knee.
Now place the ball of your left foot on the block. This will be your starting position.
Raise your toes up as high as possible as you exhale and you contract your calf muscle. Hold the contraction for a second.
Slowly return to the starting position, stretching as far down as possible.
Repeat for your prescribed number of repetitions and then repeat with the right leg.
easier_version: Use a lighter weight and a smaller range of motion.
harder_version: Use a slightly heavier load, add a pause, or slow the lowering phase.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Dumbbell Seated One-Leg Calf Raise, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Hip Flexion with Band
steps: Secure one end of the band to the lower portion of a post and attach the other to one ankle.
Face away from the attachment point of the band.
Keeping your head and your chest up, raise your knee up to 90 degrees and pause.
Return the leg to the starting position.
easier_version: Use a lighter band or stand closer to the anchor point.
harder_version: Use a stronger band, step farther from the anchor, or add a pause.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
Check the band and anchor before starting.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Hip Flexion with Band, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.

name: Standing Dumbbell Calf Raise
steps: Stand with your torso upright holding two dumbbells in your hands by your sides. Place the ball of the foot on a sturdy and stable wooden board (that is around 2-3 inches tall) while your heels extend off and touch the floor. This will be your starting position.

With the toes pointing either straight (to hit all parts equally), inwards (for emphasis on the outer head) or outwards (for emphasis on the inner head), raise the heels off the floor as you exhale by contracting the calves. Hold the top contraction for a second.
As you inhale, go back to the starting position by slowly lowering the heels.
Repeat for the recommended amount of times.
easier_version: Use a lighter weight and a smaller range of motion.
harder_version: Use a slightly heavier load, add a pause, or slow the lowering phase.
safety_notes: Use a controlled, pain-free range and stop if discomfort increases.
Reduce depth or use support if knees feel irritated.
image_prompt: Clean instructional fitness illustration for Standing Dumbbell Calf Raise, rehab knee movement, neutral background, show safe start and finish positions, no text.
